opp.name: AshleyFurniture_S2P_April'26
opp.next_step: June 17 Rick: Make the first pass shortlist in 6 weeks, present Demo, then make the final 2 list in 8 additional weeks.
opp.ais_why:
• The buyer made AI the headline driver on the May 8 2026 intro call. The Ashley team told Zycus that "Upper management's primary driver is AI", with the initial focus on AI-driven spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk. This is buyer-stated language captured live in a recorded discovery, not vendor positioning.
• The scope explicitly includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA, and Zycus has rated AI importance as 'Very Critical' - the highest tier. The buyer corroborated by asking about Teams-based Merlin Intake ("Anybody use Teams?") and probing system accuracy and flexibility for AI-driven categorization.
• Ashley is driving toward an RFP with a tight 6-to-8-week shortlist and a mid-2027 go-live, requesting demo materials and bid documents the same day. The reaction to the AI demos was strongly positive and the RFP submission is committed, indicating budget and urgency are aligned with the AI agenda.

Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 1: 'Initial phase to focus on AI-driven spend analytics, supplier onboarding, sourcing, risk and supplier performance, with phase two expanding to full indirect procure-to-pay'
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated a clear strategic driver—upper management's mandate to modernize procurement with AI—but has not yet quantified the financial or operational impact (cost savings, cycle-time reduction, error rates, or ROI). On the intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 1 stated 'Upper management's primary driver is AI; initial focus on spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk/performance', and the phased scope (Phase 1: AI-driven analytics, sourcing, onboarding, risk; Phase 2: full indirect procure-to-pay with punch-out catalogs, requisitions, approvals, PO, invoicing) signals a broad transformation. Speaker_id 3 raised a question about savings accuracy ('The analysis for the lowest unit price does not consider prices from 12 months ago, which may affect the accuracy of potential savings calculations'), showing value-case scrutiny, but no hard numbers (annual spend under management, target savings %, headcount rationalization) were shared. The next move is to quantify the business case—projected savings, process-efficiency gains, or audit-risk reduction—during the RFP response or demo phase, tying Merlin/Agentic AI capabilities (autonomous negotiation, touchless risk monitoring) to measurable outcomes the CFO or upper management will sponsor.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Matthew Roby (Senior Director Corporate Procurement), Courtney Kessel (Purchasing Category Leader), Ashley Frantz (Director of Indirect Purchasing)
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026: Matthew Roby (speaker_id 2) asked multiple questions (timeline, integration, flexibility), making him the most engaged contact
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 action item: 'Send the bid/RFP document to the Zycus team today' (promised 8 May, not yet received as of 26 Jun 2026—49 days overdue)
No evidence of advocacy behaviors (EB intro, internal coaching, competitor de-positioning, pushing RFP forward) from any contact
champion.narrative: A champion—an internal advocate with access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker who actively coaches Zycus and drives the agenda—has not yet emerged. The three Ashley contacts on record (Courtney Kessel, Purchasing Category Leader; Matthew Roby, Senior Director Corporate Procurement; Ashley Frantz, Director of Indirect Purchasing) are all procurement-side and engaged on the intro call 8 May 2026, but none has yet demonstrated advocacy behaviors (sharing internal priorities, coaching on competitor positioning, making EB introductions, or pushing the RFP forward). Matthew Roby is the most senior and most active (he asked multiple questions about timeline, integration, and flexibility on the call), making him the lead contact and a developing champion candidate, but his engagement has been transactional—discovery-phase Q&A—rather than advocacy. The RFP document promised on 8 May 2026 ('Send the bid/RFP document to the Zycus team today', speaker_id 3 action item) has not yet been delivered as of 26 Jun 2026 (49 days later), which may signal either bureaucratic delay or cooling interest; this lag is a yellow flag. Courtney Kessel and Ashley Frantz were present on the intro call but did not ask questions or drive the conversation, so their level of engagement and influence is unclear. The next step is to re-engage Matthew Roby directly, secure the overdue RFP, and ask him explicitly: who is the executive sponsor for this initiative, who will approve the budget, and how can Zycus help him build the internal business case? If Roby provides those introductions and coaches Zycus on the evaluation priorities, he will be a confirmed champion; if he remains passive or unresponsive, the deal lacks a champion and Zycus must multi-thread to find one (likely a VP Procurement or the CFO).

sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'Decision on a single vendor is targeted within the next 6–8 weeks' (multi-vendor RFP confirmed, but no competitors named)
SF Competitors__c: empty
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026: Zycus demonstrated Merlin AI capabilities (autonomous negotiation, Supplier Risk Radar, Merlin Intake, AP Smartdesk) aligned to buyer's AI mandate
competition.narrative: No competitors were named on the intro call 8 May 2026, and the Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty. The buyer is running a multi-vendor RFP ('Decision on a single vendor is targeted within the next 6–8 weeks', speaker_id 2, Avoma 8 May 2026), so competition exists but has not been disclosed. In a structured RFP for a $320K S2P platform with strong AI/Merlin emphasis, the likely peer set includes Coupa (market leader, AI/analytics strength), SAP Ariba (ERP integration, large-enterprise fit), Jaggaer (sourcing/supplier management), GEP (AI-led platform), Ivalua (flexible/configurable), and potentially Basware or Zycus's direct AI-platform peers. The buyer's emphasis on AS/400 integration, Maximo continuity, AI-driven analytics, and autonomous negotiation/risk monitoring (Merlin/Agentic AI) suggests they are evaluating platforms with strong AI differentiation and legacy-system connectors—Coupa and GEP are the most probable high-threat peers in that lens. The wedge is Merlin's agentic AI capabilities (autonomous negotiation backed by 32 patents, 100% touchless risk monitoring via the relevant stakeholder, natural-language requisition via Merlin Intake in Teams, AP Smartdesk for invoice automation), which were demonstrated on the intro call and align directly with upper management's AI mandate. The immediate move is to ask Matthew Roby (or the champion once identified) which other vendors are being evaluated, what their perceived strengths are, and how Zycus can differentiate—this intelligence is critical to tailor the demo and commercial response for the shortlist phase (due early August 2026).

Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'Is the system accurate as we implement it, and is it flexible enough to suit an individual's need or a group's collective needs?'
identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's pain is clear and strategic: upper management has mandated AI-led procurement modernization to drive efficiency, accuracy, and risk visibility across a fragmented, legacy-system environment (AS/400 ERP, Maximo for indirect/work-order purchasing, Intellidree for contracts). On the intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 1 stated 'Upper management's primary driver is AI; initial focus on spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk/performance', and speaker_id 3 emphasized the need for 'a unified front-end for both direct and indirect spend analytics and sourcing, while PO execution and catalog management would remain or integrate with systems like Maximo'. The pain owners are the procurement leadership (Matthew Roby, Senior Director; Ashley Frantz, Director Indirect; Courtney Kessel, Purchasing Category Leader) who must execute the mandate, but the ultimate pain owner is the C-level/upper-management sponsor driving the AI initiative. Speaker_id 3 also raised accuracy concerns ('The analysis for the lowest unit price does not consider prices from 12 months ago, which may affect the accuracy of potential savings calculations') and flexibility requirements ('Is the system accurate as we implement it, and is it flexible enough to suit an individual's need or a group's collective needs?'), revealing operational pain—current systems lack real-time data, AI-driven insights, and user configurability. The compelling event is the upper-management mandate and the competitive pressure to deploy AI capabilities; there is no stated external forcing function (contract expiration, compliance deadline, M&A event), but the structured timeline (go-live June 2027) signals urgency.

sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3: 'ERP is AS 400; Maximo used for indirect purchasing—Zycus tool must partner to handle work-order demand'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'ISaaS connector supports bi-directional data exchange with AS 400 flat files, notably for AP integration'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'Full Source-to-Pay implementation estimated at 5–7 months'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3: 'Target go-live June 2027'
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal redlines) and InfoSec review will follow finalist selection (late September/early October 2026 per the buyer's timeline), but the paper-process details—procurement channel (direct vs through a partner/reseller), legal review cycle, InfoSec questionnaire, IT/security approvals, ERP integration sign-off—have not been discussed. The buyer uses AS/400 ERP and Maximo for indirect purchasing/work-order demand (source: Avoma 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 'ERP is AS 400; Maximo used for indirect purchasing'), and speaker_id 2 confirmed 'ISaaS connector supports bi-directional data exchange with AS 400 flat files, notably for AP integration', so the integration scope (flat-file connectors, Maximo continuity, Intellidree contract-repository interface) will require IT/InfoSec sign-off. Implementation is estimated at 5-7 months (source: Avoma 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2), and the target go-live is June 2027, which implies contracting must complete by November 2026 to allow a December 2026 or January 2027 kickoff. No current contract expiration date was shared, and no mention of an incumbent vendor contract renewal lever. The next step is to surface the paper-process owners (legal counsel, IT/InfoSec lead, ERP/integration lead) during the shortlist or finalist phase and to map the approval chain (number of signatures, typical legal cycle time) so contracting can be de-risked before finalist selection.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Courtney Kessel (Purchasing Category Leader), Matthew Roby (Senior Director Corporate Procurement), Ashley Frantz (Director of Indirect Purchasing)—all procurement, no CFO or C-level
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 1: 'Upper management's primary driver is AI' (implies executive mandate but no named sponsor)
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3: 'Decision timeline: shortlist in 6–8 weeks, final vendor selection +8 weeks thereafter'—formal approval gate implied but EB not yet identified
economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer—the executive who controls budget approval and signs the contract—has not been identified or engaged. The intro call 8 May 2026 included three Ashley contacts (Courtney Kessel, Purchasing Category Leader; Matthew Roby, Senior Director Corporate Procurement; Ashley Frantz, Director of Indirect Purchasing), all procurement-side roles, but none held the commercial conversation or referenced CFO/C-level sign-off authority. Speaker_id 1's statement that 'Upper management's primary driver is AI' implies executive sponsorship exists at the VP or C-level, but that sponsor has not joined a call, nor has budget authority been explicitly confirmed. The Salesforce contact-role roster (3 contacts, all procurement) and the single Avoma call show no CFO, VP Finance, or Chief Procurement Officer participation. The buyer's decision timeline ('shortlist in 6–8 weeks, final vendor selection +8 weeks thereafter') suggests a formal approval gate will surface during finalist presentations, but reaching the EB before finalist selection—ideally during the shortlist demo phase—is critical to validate budget, timing, and executive sponsorship. The immediate move is to ask Matthew Roby (Senior Director, the most senior contact on record) who will approve the budget and sign the contract, and to request an executive briefing as part of the shortlist presentation.

sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3: 'Decision timeline: shortlist in 6–8 weeks, final vendor selection +8 weeks thereafter, target go-live June 2027'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'Decision on a single vendor is targeted within the next 6–8 weeks, with phased implementation to follow'

Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 action item: 'Send the bid/RFP document to the Zycus team today' (RFP not yet received as of 26 Jun 2026)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured, multi-vendor RFP process with a clear timeline: shortlist decision in 6-8 weeks from 8 May 2026 (by early August 2026), final vendor selection +8 weeks thereafter (by late September/early October 2026), followed by contracting and a target go-live of June 2027. On the intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 stated 'Decision timeline: shortlist in 6–8 weeks, final vendor selection +8 weeks thereafter, target go-live June 2027', and speaker_id 2 confirmed the multi-vendor nature by asking 'Decision on a single vendor is targeted within the next 6–8 weeks'. The rep updated NextStep on 17 Jun 2026, acknowledging the timeline: 'Make the first pass shortlist in 6 weeks, present Demo, then make the final 2 list in 8 additional weeks'. The decision sequence is: (1) RFP submission and vendor demos (current phase, overdue—RFP promised 8 May but not yet received), (2) shortlist to 2-3 vendors by early August with deeper demos/fit sessions, (3) finalist presentations and final vendor selection by late September/early October, (4) contracting (MSA/SOW), legal, InfoSec, and ERP/Maximo integration deep-dive, (5) kickoff targeting a June 2027 go-live. The process is buyer-led and methodical; the buying committee includes procurement (Kessel, Roby, Frantz) but the approval authority (likely CFO or CPO) has not yet surfaced. The current blocker is the missing RFP document, which should drive the next engagement and demo scheduling.

sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 1 and speaker_id 3: 'Phased roll-out: Phase 1 on sourcing, spend analytics, supplier onboarding, Phase 2 on catalog/procure-to-pay'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'Is the system accurate as we implement it, and is it flexible enough to suit an individual's need or a group's collective needs?'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3: 'ERP is AS 400; Maximo used for indirect purchasing—Zycus tool must partner to handle work-order demand'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 2: 'Full Source-to-Pay implementation estimated at 5–7 months; timeline adjustable by complexity and phasing'
Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 action item: 'Send the bid/RFP document to the Zycus team today' (not yet received as of 26 Jun 2026)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has defined a two-phase scope and emphasized AI/Merlin capabilities, system integration with AS/400 ERP and Maximo, and flexibility for individual and group needs—these are early decision criteria—but the formal RFP document (promised by the buyer on 8 May 2026, 'Send the bid/RFP document to the Zycus team today') has not yet been received, so the complete weighted evaluation scorecard (technical fit, pricing, implementation timeline, references, security/compliance, post-go-live support) is still unknown. On the intro call, speaker_id 1 and speaker_id 3 stated the phased approach: 'Phase 1 on sourcing, spend analytics, supplier onboarding, Phase 2 on catalog/procure-to-pay', and speaker_id 3 asked 'Is the system accurate as we implement it, and is it flexible enough to suit an individual's need or a group's collective needs?', revealing that accuracy and configurability are top-of-mind. Speaker_id 3 also questioned the AS/400 integration ('ERP is AS 400; Maximo used for indirect purchasing—Zycus tool must partner to handle work-order demand') and the Intellidree contract-repository connector, signaling integration depth as a criterion. The timeline criterion is clear: 5-7 month implementation to hit June 2027 go-live (speaker_id 2: 'Full Source-to-Pay implementation estimated at 5–7 months'). The RFP submission is overdue (promised 8 May 2026, not yet received as of 26 Jun 2026); securing it is the immediate action to lock the full criteria and tailor the demo and commercial response.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Hungry. Upper management explicitly mandated AI as the primary driver for procurement modernization ('Upper management's primary driver is AI', speaker_id 1, intro call 8 May 2026), and the two-phase scope prioritizes AI-driven spend analytics, sourcing, supplier onboarding, risk monitoring, and performance management in Phase 1, with procure-to-pay automation (catalogs, requisitions, approvals, PO, invoicing) in Phase 2. The buyer engaged deeply with Zycus's Merlin/Agentic AI capabilities on the intro call (autonomous negotiation, Supplier Risk Radar, Merlin Intake in Teams, AP Smartdesk) and asked technical questions about AI accuracy, flexibility, and historical data depth. The product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI, and the buyer's timeline (shortlist in 6-8 weeks, go-live June 2027) signals urgency to deploy AI capabilities. No AIS score is on record in Salesforce (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are null), but the call evidence and mandate unambiguously rate this buyer as AI Hungry. The shortlist demo must showcase live AI use cases (autonomous negotiation savings, real-time risk alerts, natural-language requisition) with quantified ROI to lock the AI differentiation wedge against competitors.

Buyer promised to send RFP document same day (8 May) but has not yet delivered it as of 26 Jun 2026. Rep updated NextStep 17 Jun 2026 (9 days ago) confirming the 6-week shortlist + 8-week finalist timeline; this is a rep-side touch awaiting buyer reply, not a two-way engagement. Last verified Salesforce activity 27 May 2026 (30 days ago). Stage remains Formal Evaluation; no progression since creation. Deal is live (buyer running structured RFP), but momentum has paused pending RFP delivery and next demo/engagement.

items.detail: Economic Buyer and C-level AI mandate sponsor not identified or engaged; all contact to date is with mid-level procurement (Roby, Kessel, Frantz). Budget authority, executive sponsorship, and approval chain unmapped. Risk: deal advances to finalist stage without EB validation, then stalls on budget or C-level sign-off.

Single-thread risk: no champion with EB access or advocacy behaviors; Matthew Roby is developing but passive (RFP overdue 49 days, no EB intro, no internal coaching). Risk: Zycus lacks internal intelligence on evaluation priorities, competitor positioning, and political dynamics; a competitor with a stronger champion wins on insider advantage.

Competitive blindness: multi-vendor RFP confirmed, but no competitors named. Risk: Zycus demo and commercial response are not tailored to de-position the actual peer set (likely Coupa, GEP, Ariba), and a competitor wins on better fit or pricing without Zycus knowing the comparison basis.

AS/400 ERP and Maximo integration depth (flat-file connectors, work-order demand continuity, Intellidree contract-repository interface) not yet scoped or validated with IT/InfoSec leads. Risk: technical fit assumptions break during finalist deep-dive or InfoSec review, requiring custom development or partner support that delays go-live or inflates cost.

Close date 25 Sep 2026 does not accommodate the buyer's stated decision timeline (shortlist early August, finalist late Sep/early Oct, then contracting) + 5-7 month implementation to hit June 2027 go-live. Risk: forecast miss and rep/leadership misalignment on realistic close timing.

No quantified business case (savings %, ROI, efficiency gains) shared by buyer; AI mandate is strategic but not financially anchored. Risk: CFO/EB does not sponsor the deal without hard numbers, and a competitor with a stronger ROI story wins the finalist presentation.

champion_strength.summary: No confirmed champion. Matthew Roby (Senior Director Corporate Procurement) is the most engaged contact (asked multiple questions on intro call 8 May 2026 about timeline, integration, flexibility) and the developing champion candidate, but he has not demonstrated advocacy behaviors: no Economic Buyer introduction, no internal coaching on evaluation priorities or competitor positioning, and no action to push the RFP forward (promised 8 May, overdue 49 days as of 26 Jun 2026). Courtney Kessel and Ashley Frantz were present on the intro call but passive (no questions or follow-up). The deal is single-threaded to mid-level procurement, with no access to the C-level AI mandate sponsor, the CFO/budget authority, or the IT/InfoSec/integration leads. Roby is developing but at risk of remaining transactional; the immediate move is to re-engage him directly to secure the RFP, ask for competitive intel, and request EB/executive-sponsor introductions—if he provides those, he becomes a champion; if not, multi-thread to find a new champion path.

confidence_signals.summary: Mixed signals: strong strategic fit (AI mandate, clear two-phase scope, Merlin capabilities align to buyer priorities) and a structured buyer-led RFP process signal genuine intent, but momentum has paused. The RFP document promised 8 May 2026 is 49 days overdue, and no buyer engagement has occurred since the intro call (49 days ago); the rep updated NextStep 17 Jun (9 days ago) but this is a one-way touch awaiting buyer reply, not a two-way conversation. The buyer has not initiated contact, scheduled a follow-up demo, or responded to the deliverables Zycus committed (meeting notes/recording, Showpad site, demo deck). This lag may signal bureaucratic delay in a large enterprise RFP (normal in a 6-8 week shortlist window), OR it may signal cooling interest, a higher-priority internal initiative, or a competitor gaining ground. Matthew Roby (the most engaged contact) has not followed through on the RFP, raising the risk that Zycus is not his preferred vendor or that he lacks the authority/urgency to drive the process. The deal is not cold (the structured timeline and AI mandate are real), but it is cooling. The immediate move is to re-engage Roby directly this week to diagnose the stall, secure the RFP, and confirm Zycus is still in the shortlist; if no reply within 5 business days, escalate to multi-thread (reach the executive sponsor or IT/InfoSec leads via LinkedIn/alternative channels) to restart momentum.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 91 (close 2026-09-25). Created 30 Apr 2026; 57 days old. Time in Formal Evaluation: at least 30 days (last activity 27 May 2026, latest NextStep update 17 Jun 2026). Buyer timeline from intro call 8 May 2026: shortlist decision in 6-8 weeks (by ~early August 2026), final vendor +8 weeks thereafter (~late Sep/early Oct 2026), then contracting and InfoSec, with go-live mid-2027. The forecast close date (25 Sep) lands DURING the final-vendor selection window, leaving zero time for proposal, negotiation, MSA/SOW, legal, or InfoSec review. A realistic close is late October to mid-November 2026. No forward stage progression since creation; last buyer engagement was intro call 8 May 2026 (49 days ago). Rep updated NextStep 17 Jun 2026 (9 days ago) confirming the timeline; this is a rep-side touch awaiting buyer response, not a two-way engagement. The deal is live and the buyer is running a structured RFP, but the date needs a 30-45 day push.
evidence: Close date 25 Sep 2026 does not allow time for the buyer's stated 6-8 week shortlist + 8 week final-vendor process + contracting (source: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 'Decision timeline: shortlist in 6–8 weeks, final vendor selection +8 weeks thereafter, target go-live June 2027')
Rep confirmed the timeline on 17 Jun 2026 in NextStep: 'Make the first pass shortlist in 6 weeks, present Demo, then make the final 2 list in 8 additional weeks' (source: SF NextStep 17 Jun 2026)
Last verified buyer engagement was intro call 8 May 2026 (49 days ago); rep reached out 17 Jun 2026 (9 days ago) awaiting reply (source: SF LastActivityDate 27 May, NextStep 17 Jun)
Stage Formal Evaluation is appropriate for an RFP shortlist phase; forecast category Pipeline is correct given no EB/Champion/Decision Criteria locked (source: SF StageName, ForecastCategoryName)

Strong AI appetite from upper management drives the evaluation; phased scope (Phase 1: AI analytics, sourcing, risk; Phase 2: procure-to-pay) is well-defined (source: Avoma 8 May 2026, speaker_id 1 'Upper management's primary driver is AI; initial focus on spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk/performance')
north_star_verdict.headline: Live RFP in Formal Evaluation with strong AI appetite and clear two-phase scope, but buyer engagement has gone dark for 30 days since last activity (27 May). Rep outreach 17 Jun awaits reply; no progress on shortlist decision now overdue.

best_practice_check.summary: Three high-leverage levers to raise win probability now: (1) Re-engage Matthew Roby to secure the overdue RFP and competitive intel before the shortlist demo (due early August 2026), and convert him from a transactional contact to an active champion by asking him to introduce the EB/CFO and coach Zycus on evaluation priorities. (2) Build the quantified business case (AI-driven savings, risk-reduction ROI, efficiency gains) tied to Merlin/Agentic AI capabilities, so the shortlist demo and finalist presentation land with CFO-grade financial impact, not just feature fit. (3) Multi-thread beyond procurement: identify and engage the executive sponsor (VP Procurement, CFO, or C-level AI mandate owner) and the IT/InfoSec/ERP integration leads before finalist selection, so budget authority, paper-process owners, and technical sign-off are locked early—this de-risks the post-selection contracting phase and prevents late-stage stalls.

competitive_position.summary: No competitors have been disclosed. The buyer is running a multi-vendor RFP (confirmed 8 May 2026, 'Decision on a single vendor is targeted within the next 6–8 weeks'), so competition exists but is unknown. In a $320K S2P RFP with strong AI emphasis, the probable peer set includes Coupa (market leader, AI/analytics), SAP Ariba (ERP integration), GEP (AI platform), Jaggaer (sourcing), and Ivalua (flexibility). Coupa and GEP are the most likely high-threat peers given the buyer's AI mandate and AS/400 integration requirement. Zycus's wedge is Merlin's agentic AI (autonomous negotiation with 32 patents, 100% touchless Supplier Risk Radar, natural-language Merlin Intake in Teams, AP Smartdesk invoice automation), demonstrated on the intro call 8 May 2026 and aligned directly to upper management's AI-first directive. Securing competitive intel from Matthew Roby or the champion during the shortlist phase (due early August 2026) is critical to tailor the demo and positioning.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI positioning: the buyer's upper-management mandate is explicitly AI-first ('Upper management's primary driver is AI', speaker_id 1, intro call 8 May 2026), and Zycus presented Merlin/Agentic AI capabilities (autonomous negotiation with 32 patents live ~1 year, 100% touchless Supplier Risk Radar monitoring 40+ risk types, natural-language Merlin Intake in Teams, AP Smartdesk invoice automation) on the intro call, aligning directly to the buyer's Phase 1 scope (AI-driven spend analytics, sourcing, onboarding, risk/performance). The buyer asked about AI functionality (Teams integration, accuracy, flexibility) and raised a technical question about savings-calculation depth ('lowest unit price does not consider prices from 12 months ago'), showing scrutiny but also engagement with the AI narrative. The product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI, confirming AI is central to the commercial discussion. Not under-positioned; the shortlist demo (due early August 2026) should deepen the Merlin story with live use-case scenarios (autonomous negotiation on tail spend, real-time risk alerts, natural-language requisition workflow) and quantified ROI (savings %, cycle-time reduction) to separate from feature-parity competitors (Coupa, GEP) and tie AI differentiation to the CFO-grade business case.

items.quote: Upper management's primary driver is AI; initial focus on spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk/performance
Phased roll-out: Phase 1 on sourcing, spend analytics, supplier onboarding, Phase 2 on catalog/procure-to-pay
items.criterion: Two-phase scope: Phase 1 AI analytics/sourcing/risk, Phase 2 procure-to-pay

items.quote: ERP is AS 400; Maximo used for indirect purchasing—Zycus tool must partner to handle work-order demand. ISaaS connector supports bi-directional data exchange with AS 400 flat files, notably for AP integration
items.source: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026, speaker_id 3 and speaker_id 2

items.quote: Is the system accurate as we implement it, and is it flexible enough to suit an individual's need or a group's collective needs?
items.criterion: Accuracy and flexibility for individual/group needs

items.quote: The analysis for the lowest unit price does not consider prices from 12 months ago, which may affect the accuracy of potential savings calculations
items.criterion: Historical data depth for savings calculation

items.quote: Full Source-to-Pay implementation estimated at 5–7 months; timeline adjustable by complexity and phasing
items.criterion: 5-7 month implementation to hit June 2027 go-live

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong initial fit: the buyer's AI-first mandate, two-phase scope (Phase 1: AI analytics/sourcing/risk, Phase 2: procure-to-pay), and need for AS/400 + Maximo integration align well with Zycus's Merlin/Agentic AI platform and ISaaS connector capabilities. Key fit areas confirmed on intro call 8 May 2026: AI-driven spend analytics, autonomous negotiation, touchless risk monitoring, natural-language requisition (Merlin Intake in Teams), unified front-end for direct/indirect spend, and phased rollout flexibility. Integration with AS/400 (flat-file connector) and Maximo continuity (work-order demand) is feasible per Zycus ISaaS. Buyer emphasized accuracy, flexibility, and 5-7 month implementation to hit June 2027 go-live—all achievable. One exposure: buyer questioned savings-calculation accuracy ('lowest unit price does not consider prices from 12 months ago'), signaling scrutiny of AI analytics depth; demo must address historical data coverage and benchmarking rigor. Formal RFP criteria not yet received (overdue since 8 May 2026), so complete weighted scorecard (technical, pricing, references, security, support) is still TBD.

hard.ais_why:
• The buyer made AI the headline driver on the May 8 2026 intro call. The Ashley team told Zycus that "Upper management's primary driver is AI", with the initial focus on AI-driven spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk. This is buyer-stated language captured live in a recorded discovery, not vendor positioning.
• The scope explicitly includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA, and Zycus has rated AI importance as 'Very Critical' - the highest tier. The buyer corroborated by asking about Teams-based Merlin Intake ("Anybody use Teams?") and probing system accuracy and flexibility for AI-driven categorization.
• Ashley is driving toward an RFP with a tight 6-to-8-week shortlist and a mid-2027 go-live, requesting demo materials and bid documents the same day. The reaction to the AI demos was strongly positive and the RFP submission is committed, indicating budget and urgency are aligned with the AI agenda.
